And in his hot displeasure, and in his fierce anger, in his time, will cut off those wicked, unfaithful, and unjust stewards, and appoint them their portion among hypocrites, and unbelievers;
He hath cut off in his fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy, and he burned against Jacob like a flaming fire, which devoureth round about.
And of thy mercy cut off mine enemies, and destroy all them that afflict my soul: for I am thy servant.
Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.
Because thou obeyedst not the voice of the Lord, nor executedst his fierce wrath upon Amalek, therefore hath the Lord done this thing unto thee this day.
Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
Thy wrath lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted me with all thy waves. Selah.
In that ye provoke me unto wrath with the works of your hands, burning incense unto other gods in the land of Egypt, whither ye be gone to dwell, that ye might cut yourselves off, and that ye might…
¶ Cut off thine hair, O Jerusalem, and cast it away, and take up a lamentation on high places; for the Lord hath rejected and forsaken the generation of his wrath.
And out of his mouth goeth a sharp sword, that with it he should smite the nations: and he shall rule them with a rod of iron: and he treadeth the winepress of the fierceness and wrath of Almighty…

Then said he unto me, This is the curse that goeth forth over the face of the whole earth: for every one that stealeth shall be cut off as on this side according to it; and every one that sweareth…
As when a man goeth into the wood with his neighbour to hew wood, and his hand fetcheth a stroke with the axe to cut down the tree, and the head slippeth from the helve, and lighteth upon his…
And in the day that he goeth into the sanctuary, unto the inner court, to minister in the sanctuary, he shall offer his sin offering, saith the Lord God.
They have blown the trumpet, even to make all ready; but none goeth to the battle: for my wrath is upon all the multitude thereof.
Because of the wrath of the Lord it shall not be inhabited, but it shall be wholly desolate: every one that goeth by Babylon shall be astonished, and hiss at all her plagues.
Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.
And the water ran round about the altar; and he filled the trench also with water.
¶ And he made a molten sea, ten cubits from the one brim to the other: it was round all about, and his height was five cubits: and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about.
And under the brim of it round about there were knops compassing it, ten in a cubit, compassing the sea round about: the knops were cast in two rows, when it was cast.
And in the top of the base was there a round compass of half a cubit high: and on the top of the base the ledges thereof and the borders thereof were of the same.
Lift up thine eyes round about and behold; all these gather themselves together, and they shall come to thee. And as I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt surely clothe thee with them all, as with an…
Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.

And the multitude of the earth was gathered together; and I beheld that they were in a large and spacious building, like unto the building which my father saw. And the angel of the Lord spake unto me…
In that day the Lord will take away the bravery of their tinkling ornaments, and cauls, and round tires like the moon;
They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.
And the water ran round about the altar; and he filled the trench also with water.
And as I cast my eyes round about, that perhaps I might discover my family also, I beheld a river of water; and it ran along, and it was near the tree of which I was partaking the fruit.
They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
¶ But before they lay down, the men of the city, even the men of Sodom, compassed the house round, both old and young, all the people from every quarter:
The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.
Many bulls have compassed me: strong bulls of Bashan have beset me round.
They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.

They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
In that thou lovest thine enemies, and hatest thy friends. For thou hast declared this day, that thou regardest neither princes nor servants: for this day I perceive, that if Absalom had lived, and…
All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.
And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?
Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me.

Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you, my friends Sidney and Joseph, your families are well; they are in mine hands, and I will do with them as seemeth me good; for in me there is all power.
And as I said unto mine apostles, even so I say unto you, for you are mine apostles, even God’s high priests; ye are they whom my Father hath given me; ye are my friends;
My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.
Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you, my friends Sidney and Joseph, your families are well; they are in mine hands, and I will do with them as seemeth me good; for in me there is all power.
Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God. I am the same that came unto mine own, and mine own received me not. I am the light which shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not.
And as I said unto mine apostles, even so I say unto you, for you are mine apostles, even God’s high priests; ye are they whom my Father hath given me; ye are my friends;
If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.
He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.
Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: I am shut up, and I cannot come forth.
I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
But it was thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.
And again, verily I say unto you, and I say it that you may know the truth, that you may chase darkness from among you;
Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God. I am the same that came unto mine own, and mine own received me not. I am the light which shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not.
If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.
And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.
